items.correct: Increases red blood cell production, improving oxygen-carrying capacity.
items.why: Students assume all PEDs work the same way. EPO is specifically for endurance - more red cells = more oxygen to working muscles. Anabolic steroids do something different (promote protein synthesis).

items.q: What type of joint is the knee?
items.why: Students confuse knee with hip. Both are in the leg, but the hip is ball-and-socket (multi-directional); the knee is a hinge (mostly flexion-extension). Ball-and-socket joints have more range.

items.q: What does the 'I' in FITT stand for?
items.why: Students guess. FITT = Frequency, Intensity, Time, Type - the variables you adjust to apply progressive overload. Intensity = how hard (e.g., % of 1RM or heart rate).

items.q: Spot-fixing differs from match-fixing in that:
items.correct: It targets specific in-play events, not the overall result
items.why: It's It targets specific in-play events, not the overall result. “It is legal” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
Correct answer: Media payments for exclusive coverage. “Spectator gate receipts” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: If Premier League TV deal is $13 bn over 3 years, annual value ($ bn yr⁻¹) is?
items.why: The real figure is 4.33. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
